response.justification: While the candidate has extensive software development experience and strong engineering discipline, they are a poor fit for this role. Most critically, there is no evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is explicitly required. Additionally, their recent 10+ years of experience focus on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React), whereas the position requires embedded systems security expertise. Their C/UNIX experience appears outdated (last C role in 2015), and there is no demonstrated background in security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), network switching, or Linux kernel work essential to the role.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across diverse platforms and domains
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and mentoring capabilities
Demonstrated ability to learn new technologies and work with multiple programming languages
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency listed (explicitly required qualification)
Recent career focused on web and full-stack development; embedded systems expertise is limited and dated (2015)
No demonstrable background in required security technologies, network protocols (SNMP, NETCONF), or Linux kernel-level work
